speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Once more we sit in England’s royal throne,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Repurchased with the blood of enemies.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: What valiant foemen, like to autumn’s corn,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Have we mowed down in tops of all their pride!

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Three dukes of Somerset, threefold renowned

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: For hardy and undoubted champions;

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Two Cliffords, as the father and the son;

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And two Northumberlands; two braver men

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Ne’er spurred their coursers at the trumpet’s sound.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: With them the two brave bears, Warwick and Montague,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: That in their chains fettered the kingly lion

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And made the forest tremble when they roared.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Thus have we swept suspicion from our seat

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And made our footstool of security.—

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Come hither, Bess, and let me kiss my boy.—

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Young Ned, for thee, thine uncles and myself

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Have in our armors watched the winter’s night,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Went all afoot in summer’s scalding heat,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: That thou mightst repossess the crown in peace,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And of our labors thou shalt reap the gain.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I’ll blast his harvest, if your head were laid;

speaker: RICHARD
text: For yet I am not looked on in the world.

speaker: RICHARD
text: This shoulder was ordained so thick to heave,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And heave it shall some weight or break my back.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Work thou the way and that shalt execute.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Clarence and Gloucester, love my lovely queen,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And kiss your princely nephew, brothers both.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: The duty that I owe unto your Majesty

speaker: CLARENCE
text: I seal upon the lips of this sweet babe.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Thanks, noble Clarence; worthy brother, thanks.

speaker: RICHARD
text: And that I love the tree from whence thou sprang’st,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Witness the loving kiss I give the fruit.

speaker: RICHARD
text:
Aside
                .
                 
              To say the truth, so Judas kissed his master

speaker: RICHARD
text: And cried “All hail!” whenas he meant all harm.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Now am I seated as my soul delights,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Having my country’s peace and brothers’ loves.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: What will your Grace have done with Margaret?

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Reignier, her father, to the King of France

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Hath pawned the Sicils and Jerusalem,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: And hither have they sent it for her ransom.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Away with her, and waft her hence to France.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And now what rests but that we spend the time

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: With stately triumphs, mirthful comic shows,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Such as befits the pleasure of the court?

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Sound drums and trumpets! Farewell, sour annoy,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: For here I hope begins our lasting joy.
